channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: 24 dead as fire crews try to corral Los Angeles blazes before winds return this week
item.description: Firefighters scrambled Sunday to make further progress against wildfires that have destroyed thousands of homes and killed 24 people in the Los Angeles area as forecasters again warned of dangerous weather with the return of strong winds this week.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: What to know about the devastation from the Los Angeles-area fires
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Fires ripping through the Los Angeles area have killed at least 24 people, displaced thousands of others and destroyed more than 12,000 structures while burning through an area larger than the city of San Francisco.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Incarcerated firefighters join California wildfire battle efforts
item.description: The California Department of Corrections and Rehabilitation has deployed 931 incarcerated firefighters and 114 support staff through its Fire Camp Program.
item.dc:creator: Jorge Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Fencing, roof of Anchorage pedestrian bridge fall in strong wind, closing highway
item.description: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (AP) — The side fencing and roof of a pedestrian bridge fell in strong wind early Sunday, closing the main highway south of Anchorage in both directions until the debris could be cleared.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Steve Bannon says he will ‘take down’ the ‘truly evil’ Elon Musk
item.description: “I will have Elon Musk run out of here by Inauguration Day,” Bannon said, adding “He is a truly evil guy, a very bad guy. I made it my personal thing to take this guy down.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Canada deploying 60 firefighters to help with California blazes
item.description: In a post on X, Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau said Canada was helping aid the Los Angeles area as several wildfires continue devastating the area.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Rahm Emanuel steps down as Japan ambassador ahead of Trump inauguration
item.description: “Last year, I spoke at the U.S. Naval War College and found it both rewarding and reaffirming,” Emanuel said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: No pants? No problem. London subway riders bare their legs to brighten a gray winter day
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Mind the pants. Hundreds of Londoners headed down to the Underground on Sunday afternoon, stripped down to their underwear and travelled around a bit, trying to look as though nothing unusual was going on. As if.
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Vance says Jan. 6 participants who committed violence 'obviously' shouldn't be pardoned
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Vice President-elect JD Vance says people responsible for the violence during the Capitol riot “obviously” should not be pardoned, as President-elect Donald Trump is promising to use his clemency power on behalf of many of those who tried on Jan.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: A dad and daughter help evacuees learn whether their homes survived the California wildfires
item.description: A father-daughter team in the Los Angeles area are staying in their home behind the fire barricades and taking inventory of destroyed properties.
item.dc:creator: Gene Johnson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Los Angeles burglary suspect disguised as firefighter, as looting arrests reach about 29 people: sheriff
item.description: Los Angeles County Sheriff Robert Luna said a burglary suspect disguised as a firefighter, as police arrested about 29 for looting and other criminal activity.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hurricane Katrina victim forced to evacuate again, this time for Los Angeles wildfires
item.description: The California wildfires are hitting too close to home for a woman who used to live in New Orleans. She was forced to evacuate during Hurricane Katrina, and now she's evacuating once again.
item.dc:creator: Kenny Lopez, WGN 9

item.title: New England Patriots hire former Super Bowl champ Mike Vrabel as next coach
item.description: Vrabel, a fan favorite during eight seasons as a player in New England where he was a member of its first three Super Bowl winners and an inductee into the team’s Hall of Fame in 2023, was the preferred candidate because of his long association with the franchise and coaching success.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Man says two of his cats died after drinking raw milk recalled for bird flu
item.description: “It’s horrible when you realize that you’re the one that actually gave them the milk that killed them.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Los Angeles tree advocates hope their vital work recovers from devastating fires
item.description: Some people in Los Angeles have worked for years to increase the number of trees that provide shade and respite from heat and air pollution.
item.dc:creator: Tammy Webber And Dorany Pineda, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Tennessee man arrested after shooting at family sledding in the snow, deputies say
item.description: A Tennessee man was arrested on charges of aggravated assault after he allegedly opened fire on a family that was sledding in the snow, deputies said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Los Angeles County medical examiner's office confirms wildfire death toll rises to 16
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The Los Angeles County medical examiner’s office confirmed the death toll from the wildfires ravaging the area has risen to 16. The total of confirmed fatalities stands at 16 victims, and the cases remain under investigation.
item.dc:creator: JAE HONG, HOLLY RAMER and MICHAEL R. BLOOD, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Everything you need to know about the water drops in California during wildfires
item.description: Firefighters in California are battling the fires from the sky using a variety of specialized aircraft that dump water and fire retardant onto the terrain below.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: PHOTOS: West Virginia father-son duo build ice dragon igloo
item.description: While many of us were staying cozy indoors, a father-son duo was hard at work making the best of this week's wintery weather by building a one-of-a-kind igloo.
item.dc:creator: Sam Gorski, WGN 9

item.title: LA fire chief says city failed department amid ongoing wildfires
item.description: Crowley said that when firefighters operate in the field, they expect to have water, but if there isn’t any available, “they’re going to go find water.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Biden gives Pope Francis highest civilian honor before leaving office
item.description: Biden, who is only the second Catholic president in U.S. history, spoke with the Pope by phone on Saturday to tell him he was a recipient of the medal, which is the highest civilian honor.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Powerful winter storm that dumped snow in US South maintains its icy grip
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Flight cancellations piled up and officials warned of continuing dangerous roads Saturday in the wake of a winter storm that brought biting cold and wet snow to the U.S. South, leading to school closures and disrupting travel.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY and BEN FINLEY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Walmart recalls 12,000 cartons of chicken broth
item.description: Walmart has recalled over 12,000 cartons of Great Value Family Size Chicken Broth due to packaging issues that could lead to spoilage, according to the Food and Drug Administration (FDA).
item.dc:creator: Reagan Wynn, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Winter weather road tips: Staying safe while navigating the snow and ice
item.description: CHICAGO (WGN) — Snowfall brings a whole bundle of headaches for drivers, but there are some steps that can be taken to avoid lingering issues or a potentially dangerous situation.
item.dc:creator: Gabriel Castillo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Gun jams as woman tries to shoot boyfriend: Tennessee police
item.description: A Tennessee woman is facing an attempted murder charge after reportedly attempting to shoot her boyfriend before her gun jammed, Memphis Police said.
item.dc:creator: Raven Moore, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Santa Anita postpones weekend racing as track is staging area for fire relief efforts
item.description: A flareup of the wildfire on the west side of Los Angeles that prompted new evacuations has caused Santa Anita to cancel horse racing this weekend.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: FEMA Fire admin: No amount of resources could've stopped LA blazes
item.description: "With every resource brought to bear on this particular fire no one was getting in front of (it)," Moore-Merrell said.
item.dc:creator: Matt Spears, NewsNation

item.title: Biden takes unexpected, impromptu questions from press in final days
item.description: President Biden opted Friday to voluntarily take a host of questions from the press after delivering remarks on the day’s job report, a speech that had been put on the White House schedule last minute.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Texas, Arizona arming Mexican drug cartels
item.description: The U.S. and Mexico are working closer than ever to trace the origin of guns used in drug cartel massacres and other crimes south of the border.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, Fox 5

item.title: LA Mayor Karen Bass faces critical leadership test as questions emerge about wildfire response
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Earlier this week, as hurricane-strength winds blew through bone-dry hillside subdivisions, Los Angeles saw its worst nightmare realized as long-predicted firestorms engulfed wide swaths of the nation's second-largest city.
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS RICCARDI and MICHAEL R. BLOOD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Navy veteran cleared of conspiracy charges in high-profile Jan. 6 case is sentenced to time served
item.description: A retired Navy intelligence officer who was cleared of the most serious charges in his U.S. Capitol attack trial alongside Oath Keepers founder Stewart Rhodes avoided additional time behind bars when he was sentenced on Friday.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Disney pledges $15 million in Los Angeles fire aid as more stars learn they've lost their homes
item.description: Disney says it will donate $15 million to respond to the fires and help rebuild. The company announced the donations Friday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: How a week of weather extremes upended the lives of millions of Americans
item.description: The week began with a biting cold snap that hammered much of the U.S., the product of a jet stream that slips out of its usual path more often these days.
item.dc:creator: Melina Walling, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Newsom calls for investigation into water supply after tanks 'went dry' during wildfire
item.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom has ordered an investigation into the Los Angeles Department of Water and Power amid reports of a loss of water pressure to fire hydrants and limited water resources in the wildfire zones.
item.dc:creator: Eytan Wallace, Fox 5

item.title: Florida woman messages investigator instead of drug dealer by mistake
item.description: A woman landed herself in hot water when she texted someone she believed was a drug dealer, but accidentally messaged a Bay County narcotics investigator instead.
item.dc:creator: Alaina Papazian, Fox 5

item.title: Supreme Court seems likely to uphold law that would force TikTok to shut down on Jan. 19
item.description: The Supreme Court on Friday seemed likely to uphold a law that would ban TikTok in the United States beginning Jan. 19 unless the popular social media program is sold by its China-based parent company.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9
